# Amazon EC2 Connector - Full Documentation

# Amazon EC2 Connector 3.0 Example

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

This example creates an Amazon EBS (Elastic Block Store) volume that you can attach to an Amazon EC2
instance that is in the same availability zone as the volume.

The example includes configuring global elements for Anypoint Connector for Amazon EC2 (Amazon EC2
Connector), configuring additional properties such as volume size and type, configuring the **Set
Payload** component, adding and configuring the **Logger** component, and testing your applicationâs
use of the connector.

The following image shows the Mule flow for this example:

![Flow for creating an EBS volume](_images/amazon-ec2-create-volume-usecase-flow.png)

This example uses variables for some field values. You can either:

- Replace the variables with their values in the code.
- Provide the values for each variable in a properties file and then refer to that file from the
  connector configuration.  
  If you donât know how to use a properties file, see
  [Configuring Property Placeholders](../../mule-runtime/latest/mule-app-properties-to-configure).

## Configure Global Elements for the Connector

1.  Create a new Mule project in Anypoint Studio.
2.  Create a file named `mule-artifact.properties` to store your Amazon credentials.
3.  Add the following properties to the `mule-artifact.properties` file:

    ```text
    amazon.accesskey=<Access Key>
    amazon.secretkey=<Secret Key>
    amazon.region=<Region>
    amazon.sessionToken=<Session Token>
    ```

4.  Save the `mule-artifact.properties` file in the projectâs `src/main/resources` directory.
5.  Drag the **HTTP Listener** component to the canvas.
6.  Add a global element for **HTTP Listener**:
    1.  Select **Listener** on the canvas.
    2.  In the configuration screen for the operation, click the plus sign (**+**) next to the
        **Connector configuration** field to access the global element configuration fields.
    3.  In the **Global Element Properties** dialog, provide the following values:
        - **Name**  
          `Listener`
        - **Host**  
          `localhost`
        - **Port**  
          `8081`

          The following image shows an example of the **HTTP Listener** global element
          configuration:

          ![Global element configuration for HTTP Listener](_images/http-listener-config.png)

    4.  Click **OK**.

7.  Drag the Amazon EC2 Connector **Create volume** operation next to the **Listener** component.
8.  Add a new Amazon EC2 global element:
    1.  Select the **Create volume** operation on the canvas.
    2.  In the configuration screen for the operation, click the plus sign (**+**) next to the
        **Connector configuration** field to access the global element configuration fields.

9.  Configure the global element as follows:
    - **Name**  
      `Amazon EC2 Configuration`
    - **Session Token**  
      Session token used to validate the temporary security credentials
    - **Access Key**  
      Alphanumeric text string that uniquely identifies the user who owns the account
    - **Secret Key**

      Key that acts as the password

    - **Region Endpoint**  
      Region to select from the drop-down menu for the Amazon EC2 client

      The following image shows an example of what the configuration looks like:

      ![EC2 global configuration](_images/mule-amazon-ec2-global-config.png)

      The corresponding XML looks like this:

      ```xml
      <ec2:config
      	name="Amazon_EC2_configuration"
      	doc:name="Amazon EC2 Configuration" >
      <ec2:basic-connection
      	accessKey="${amazon.accesskey}"
      	secretKey="${amazon.secretkey}"
      	region="${amazon.region}"
      	sessionToken="${amazon.sessionToken}"/>
      </ec2:config>
      ```

10. Click **Test Connection** to confirm that Mule can connect with the Amazon EC2 instance.
11. If the connection is successful, click **OK** to save the configurations.

    Otherwise, review or correct any incorrect parameters, then test again.

## Configure Additional Properties

1.  Select the **Create volume** operation on the Studio canvas.
2.  In the **General** tab, enter the following values:
    1.  Enter a value for **Display Name**.  
        This example uses `Create volume`.
    2.  In the **Basic Settings** section, select the name of the Amazon EC2 configuration you
        created in [Configure Global Elements for the Connector](amazon-ec2-connector-studio).
    3.  In the **General** section, enter values for the following fields:
        - **Availability Zone**

          Amazon availability zone you have access to. This example uses `us-east-1a`.

        - **Size**

          Size of the volume in gibibytes (GiBs). This example uses `5`.

        - **Volume Type**

          The default volume type is **Standard**.

          ![Publish message connector props](_images/amazon-ec2-create-volume-props.png)

3.  Verify that your XML looks like this:

    ```xml
    <ec2:create-volume
    	config-ref="Amazon_EC2_configuration"
    	availabilityZone="us-east-1a"
    	doc:name="Create volume"
    	size="5"/>
    ```

## Configure the Set Payload Component

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, select **Set Payload** and drag it to the right of the Amazon EC2
    **Create volume** operation.  
    The **Set Payload** transformer sends the response to the client in the browser.
2.  In the canvas, select **Set Payload** and set the **Value** field to
    `#[payload.volume.volumeId]`.

    This prints the volume ID of the EBS volume.

    The following image shows the **Set Payload** configuration:

    ![EC2 Create Volume payload transformer](_images/amazon-ec2-create-volume-payload.png)

## Add and Configure the Logger Component

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, select **Logger** and drag it to the right of the **Set Payload**
    component.  
    The **Logger** component displays the volume ID that is transformed by the **Set Payload**
    component from the **Create Volume** operation in the Mule console.
2.  In the canvas, select **Logger** and enter the following values:
    - **Message**  
      `#[payload]`
    - **Level**  
      `INFO`

      ![EC2 create volume logger](_images/amazon-ec2-create-volume-logger-props.png)

3.  Save the project.

## Test the App

1.  In **Package Explorer**, right-click the project and select **Run As > Mule Application**.
2.  Browse to `http://localhost:8081/createVolume`.

    You see the generated volume ID in the browser and console.

### Example Mule App XML Code

Copy and paste this code into your XML Editor to quickly load the flow for this example use case
into your Mule app:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

<mule xmlns:ec2="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ec2"
	xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
	xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
	xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
	xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
	xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ec2
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ec2/current/mule-ec2.xsd">
	<http:listener-config
		name="HTTP_Listener_config"
		doc:name="HTTP Listener config" >
		<http:listener-connection host="localhost" port="8081" />
	</http:listener-config>
	<ec2:config
		name="Amazon_EC2_configuration"
		doc:name="Amazon EC2 Configuration" >
		<ec2:basic-connection
			accessKey="${amazon.accesskey}"
			secretKey="${amazon.secretkey}"
			region="USEAST1"
			sessionToken="${amazon.sessionToken}"/>
	</ec2:config>
	<flow name="create-ebs-volume" >
		<http:listener
			config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config"
			path="/createVolume"
			doc:name="Listener" />
		<ec2:create-volume
			config-ref="Amazon_EC2_configuration"
			availabilityZone="us-east-1a"
			doc:name="Create volume"
			size="5"/>
		<set-payload
			value="#[payload.volume.volumeId]"
			doc:name="Set Payload"  />
		<logger
			level="INFO"
			doc:name="Logger"
			message="#[payload]"/>
	</flow>
</mule>
```

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Amazon EC2 Connector 3.0 Reference

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

## Configurations

---

### Configuration

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#config_basic">Basic</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#config_role">Role</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The connection types to provide to this configuration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Expiration Policy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ExpirationPolicy">Expiration Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the minimum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before the runtime considers it eligible for expiration. This does not mean that the platform expires the instance at the exact moment that it becomes eligible. The runtime purges the instances when appropriate.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Connection Types

##### Basic

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Session Token</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The session token provided by Amazon STS.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The amount of time to wait (in milliseconds) when initially establishing a connection before the connector gives up and times out. A value of 0 means infinity and is not recommended.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>50000</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Timeout Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Connection Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Connections</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Sets the maximum number of allowed open HTTP connections.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Socket Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The amount of time to wait (in milliseconds) for data to be transferred over an established, open connection before the connection times out. A value of 0 means infinity and is not recommended.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>50000</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Socket Timeout Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Socket Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Access Key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The access key provided by Amazon.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Secret Key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The secret key provided by Amazon.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Try Default AWSCredentials Provider Chain</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Set this field to true to obtain credentials from the AWS environment. See: <a href="https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sdk-for-java/latest/developer-guide/credentials.html">https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sdk-for-java/latest/developer-guide/credentials.html</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Region Endpoint</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Set the topic region endpoint.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>US_EAST_1</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Custom Service Endpoint</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Sets a custom service endpoint. Useful when a non-standard service endpoint is required, such as a VPC endpoint.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>TLS Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to true, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Host</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The optional proxy host.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Port</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The optional proxy port.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Username</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The optional proxy username.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The optional proxy password.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Domain</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The optional proxy domain.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Workstation</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The optional proxy workstation.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

##### Role

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Role ARN</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Role ARN that uniquely identifies the role to assume to gain cross-account access.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Custom STS Endpoint</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Sets a custom STS endpoint. Useful when a non-standard service endpoint is required, such as a VPC endpoint.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Duration (in milliseconds) when initially establishing a connection before the connector gives up and times out. A value of 0 means infinity and is not recommended.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>50000</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Timeout Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Connection Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Connections</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Sets the maximum number of allowed open HTTP connections.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Socket Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Duration (in milliseconds) to transfer data over an established, open connection before the connection times out. A value of 0 means infinity and is not recommended.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>50000</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Socket Timeout Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Socket Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Access Key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Access key provided by Amazon.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Secret Key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Secret key provided by Amazon.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Try Default AWSCredentials Provider Chain</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Set this field to true to obtain credentials from the AWS environment. See: <a href="https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sdk-for-java/latest/developer-guide/credentials.html">https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sdk-for-java/latest/developer-guide/credentials.html</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Region Endpoint</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Set the topic region endpoint.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>US_EAST_1</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Custom Service Endpoint</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Sets a custom service endpoint. Useful when a non-standard service endpoint is required, such as a VPC endpoint.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>TLS Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to true, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Host</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Optional proxy host.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Port</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Optional proxy port.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Username</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Optional proxy username.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Optional proxy password.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Domain</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Optional proxy domain.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Workstation</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Optional proxy workstation.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

- [Reset Network Interface Attribute](#resetNetworkInterfaceAttribute)
- [Reset Snapshot Attribute](#resetSnapshotAttribute)
- [Restore Address To Classic](#restoreAddressToClassic)
- [Revoke Security Group Egress](#revokeSecurityGroupEgress)
- [Revoke Security Group Ingress](#revokeSecurityGroupIngress)
- [Run Instances](#runInstances)
- [Start Instances](#startInstances)
- [Stop Instances](#stopInstances)
- [Terminate Instances](#terminateInstances)
- [Unassign IPv6 Addresses](#unassignIpv6Addresses)
- [Unassign Private IP Addresses](#unassignPrivateIpAddresses)
- [Unmonitor Instances](#unmonitorInstances)

## Operations

### Allocate Address

`<ec2:allocate-address>`

Acquires an elastic IP address.

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Domain</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Set to <code>vpc</code> to allocate the address for use with instances in a VPC. By default, if the Region supports EC2-Classic, this value is <code>standard</code>. Valid values are <code>standard</code> and <code>vpc</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>standard</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of a variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#AllocateAddressResult">Allocate Address Result</a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RequestIDAttribute">Request ID Attribute</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

See [Exception Throw List - 1](#exceptionthrows1)

### Assign IPv6 Addresses

`<ec2:assign-ipv6-addresses>`

Assigns one or more IPv6 addresses to the specified network interface.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Network Interface Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The ID of the network interface.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Ipv6 Address Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The number of IPv6 addresses to assign to the network interface. Amazon EC2 automatically selects the IPv6 addresses from the subnet range. You canât use this option if specifying specific IPv6 addresses.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Ipv6 Addresses</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>One or more specific IPv6 addresses to be assigned to the network interface. You canât use this option if youâre specifying a number of IPv6 addresses.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#AssignIpv6AddressesResult">Assign IPv6 Addresses Result</a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RequestIDAttribute">Request ID Attribute</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

See [Exception Throw List - 1](#exceptionthrows1)

### Assign Private IP Addresses

`<ec2:assign-private-ip-addresses>`

Assigns one or more secondary private IP addresses to the specified network interface.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Network Interface Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The ID of the network interface.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Allow Reassignment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates whether to allow an IP address that is already assigned to another network interface or instance to be reassigned to the specified network interface.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>false</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Private Ip Addresses</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>One or more IP addresses to be assigned as a secondary private IP address to the network interface. You canât specify this parameter when also specifying a number of secondary IP addresses.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Secondary Private Ip Address Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The number of secondary IP addresses to assign to the network interface. You canât specify this parameter when also specifying private IP addresses.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

See [Exception Throw List - 1](#exceptionthrows1)

### Associate Address

`<ec2:associate-address>`

Associates an Elastic IP address with an instance or a network interface. Associating an Elastic IP
address with an interface in a different network border group is not allowed.

> [!NOTE] This operation is idempotent. If you perform the operation more than once, an error is not
> returned, and each time the Elastic IP address is remapped to the same instance, you might be
> charged.

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Allocation Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>EC2-VPC - The allocation ID. This is required for EC2-VPC.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Allow Reassociation</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>EC2-VPC - For a VPC in an EC2-Classic account, specify <code>true</code> to allow an Elastic IP address that is already associated with an instance or network interface to be reassociated with the specified instance or network interface. Otherwise, the operation fails.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>false</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Instance Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The ID of the instance.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Network Interface Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>EC2-VPC - ID of the network interface.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Private Ip Address</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>EC2-VPC - Primary or secondary private IP address to associate with the Elastic IP address.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Public Ip</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Elastic IP address. This is required for EC2-Classic.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RequestIDAttribute">Request ID Attribute</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

See [Exception Throw List - 1](#exceptionthrows1)

### Associate IAM Instance Profile

`<ec2:associate-iam-instance-profile>`

Associates an IAM instance profile with a running or stopped instance. Associating more than one IAM
instance profile with an instance is not allowed.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Instance Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID of the instance.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Profile Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the IAM instance profile.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#IamInstanceProfileAssociation">IAM Instance Profile Association</a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RequestIDAttribute">Request ID Attribute</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

See [Exception Throw List - 1](#exceptionthrows1)

### Attach Network Interface

`<ec2:attach-network-interface>`

Attaches a network interface to an instance.

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Device Index</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Index of the device for the network interface attachment.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Instance Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID of the instance.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Network Interface Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID of the network interface.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RequestIDAttribute">Request ID Attribute</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Attach Volume

`<ec2:attach-volume>`

Attaches an EBS volume to a running or stopped instance and exposes it to the instance with the
specified device name. Encrypted EBS volumes can be attached only to instances that support Amazon
EBS encryption.

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Volume Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The ID of the EBS volume.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Instance Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The ID of the instance.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Device</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The device name to expose to the instance (for example, /dev/sdh or xvdh).</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#VolumeAttachment">Volume Attachment</a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RequestIDAttribute">Request ID Attribute</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

See [Exception Throw List - 1](#exceptionthrows1)

### Authorize Security Group Egress

`<ec2:authorize-security-group-egress>`

(EC2-VPC only) Adds one or more egress rules to a security group to use with a VPC.

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Group Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The ID of the security group.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Cidr Ip</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The CIDR IPv4 address range.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Ip Protocol</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The IP protocol name or number.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>From Port</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The start of port range for the TCP and UDP protocols, or an ICMP type number.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>To Port</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The end of port range for the TCP and UDP protocols, or an ICMP type number.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Ip Permissions</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#IpPermission">IP Permission</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Sets of IP permissions.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Source Security Group Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of a destination security group.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Source Security Group Owner Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The AWS account number for a destination security group.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

See [Exception Throw List - 1](#exceptionthrows1)

### Authorize Security Group Ingress

`<ec2:authorize-security-group-ingress>`

Adds one or more ingress rules to a security group.

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Group Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID or name of the security group. For security groups in a non-default VPC, you must specify the group ID (rather than the Group Name).</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Group Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the name of the security group for EC2-Classic and default VPC.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Cidr Ip</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>IPv4 address range in CIDR format.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Ip Protocol</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>IP protocol name (<code>tcp</code>, <code>udp</code>, <code>icmp</code>) or number. For <code>icmpv6</code>, specify a set of IP permissions.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>From Port</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Start of port range for the TCP and UDP protocols, or an ICMP/ICMPv6 type number.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>To Port</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>End of port range for the TCP and UDP protocols, or an ICMP/ICMPv6 code number.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Ip Permissions</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#IpPermission">IP Permission</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A set of IP permissions.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Source Security Group Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>EC2-Classic, default VPC - The name of the source security group.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Source Security Group Owner Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>In EC2-Classic, the AWS account number for the source security group, if the source security group is in a different account. You canât specify this parameter in combination with the CIDR IP address range, IP protocol, Start and End of port range parameters.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Throws

### Copy Snapshot

`<ec2:copy-snapshot>`

Copies a point-in-time snapshot of an EBS volume and stores it in Amazon S3. You can copy the
snapshot within the same region, or from one region to another. You can use the snapshot to create
EBS volumes or Amazon Machine Images (AMIs).

Copies of encrypted EBS snapshots remain encrypted and copies of unencrypted snapshots remain
unencrypted. To encrypt unencrypted snapshots, specify the `Encrypted` parameter during the copy
snapshot operation. Encrypted snapshot copies use the default AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS)
customer key (CMK). You can use the `KmsKeyId` parameter to specify a non-default CMK. To copy an
encrypted snapshot that is shared from another account, you must have permissions for the CMK that
is used to encrypt the snapshot.

Snapshots that are created by the `copySnapshot` action have a random volume ID that must not be
used for any other purpose.

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Source Snapshot Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID of the EBS snapshot to copy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Source Region</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>APNORTHEAST1</p></li><li><p>APNORTHEAST2</p></li><li><p>APSOUTH1</p></li><li><p>APSOUTHEAST1</p></li><li><p>APSOUTHEAST2</p></li><li><p>CACENTRAL1</p></li><li><p>EUCENTRAL1</p></li><li><p>EUWEST1</p></li><li><p>EUWEST2</p></li><li><p>SAEAST1</p></li><li><p>USEAST1</p></li><li><p>USEAST2</p></li><li><p>USWEST1</p></li><li><p>USWEST2</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID of the region that contains the snapshot to copy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Description</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Description for the EBS snapshot.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Destination Region</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String. Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>APNORTHEAST1</p></li><li><p>APNORTHEAST2</p></li><li><p>APSOUTH1</p></li><li><p>APSOUTHEAST1</p></li><li><p>APSOUTHEAST2</p></li><li><p>CACENTRAL1</p></li><li><p>EUCENTRAL1</p></li><li><p>EUWEST1</p></li><li><p>EUWEST2</p></li><li><p>SAEAST1</p></li><li><p>USEAST1</p></li><li><p>USEAST2</p></li><li><p>USWEST1</p></li><li><p>USWEST2</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Destination region to use in the <code>PresignedUrl</code> parameter of a copy snapshot operation. The snapshot is copied to the regional endpoint to which you send the HTTP request.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Presigned Url</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The pre-signed URL that facilitates copying an encrypted snapshot.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Encrypted</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies whether the destination snapshot should be encrypted.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>false</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Kms Key Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Full ARN of the AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) CMK to use when creating the snapshot copy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RequestIDAttribute">Request ID Attribute</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

See [Exception Throw List - 1](#exceptionthrows1)

### Create Key Pair

`<ec2:create-key-pair>`

Creates a new 2048-bit RSA key pair with the specified name. The public key is stored by Amazon EC2
and the private key is returned to you. The private key is returned as an unencrypted PEM-encoded
PKCS#8 private key. If a key with the specified name already exists, Amazon EC2 returns an error.

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Unique name for the key pair.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#KeyPair">Key Pair</a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RequestIDAttribute">Request ID Attribute</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Create Network Interface

`<ec2:create-network-interface>`

Creates a network interface in the specified subnet.

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Subnet Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The ID of the subnet to associate with the network interface.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Description</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A description for the network interface.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Groups</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The IDs of one or more security groups.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Ipv6 Address Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The number of IPv6 addresses to assign to a network interface. Amazon EC2 automatically selects the IPv6 addresses from the subnet range.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Ipv6 Addresses</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>One or more specific IPv6 addresses from the IPv6 CIDR block range of your subnet. You canât use this option if youâre specifying a number of IPv6 addresses.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Private Ip Address</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The primary private IPv4 address of the network interface.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Private Ip Addresses</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#PrivateIpAddressSpecification">Private IP Address Specification</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>One or more private IPv4 addresses.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Secondary Private Ip Address Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The number of secondary private IPv4 addresses to assign to a network interface.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Create Security Group

`<ec2:create-security-group>`

Creates a security group.

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Vpc Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID of the VPC.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Group Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the security group.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Description</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Description for the security group.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. ### For Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

See [Exception Throw List - 1](#exceptionthrows1)

### Create Snapshot

`<ec2:create-snapshot>`

Creates a snapshot of an EBS volume and stores it in Amazon S3. You can use snapshots for backups,
to make copies of EBS volumes, and to save data before shutting down an instance. When a snapshot is
created, any AWS Marketplace product codes that are associated with the source volume are propagated
to the snapshot.

You can take a snapshot of an attached volume that is in use, but snapshots capture only the data
that is written to your EBS volume at the time the snapshot command is issued. Data that is cached
by any apps or the operating system is excluded.

Your snapshot should be complete if you can pause file systems on the volume long enough to take the
snapshot.

If you canât pause all file writes to the volume, to ensure a consistent and complete snapshot, do
the following:

1.  Unmount the volume from within the instance.
2.  Issue the snapshot command.
3.  Remount the volume.  
    You can remount and use your volume while the snapshot status is pending.

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Volume Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID of the EBS volume.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Description</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Description for the snapshot.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#Snapshot">Snapshot</a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RequestIDAttribute">Request ID Attribute</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

See [Exception Throw List - 1](#exceptionthrows1)

### Create Tags

`<ec2:create-tags>`

Adds or overwrites one or more tags for the specified Amazon EC2 resources. Each resource can have a
maximum of 50 tags. Each tag consists of a key and optional value. Tag keys must be unique per
resource.

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Resources</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>List of IDs of one or more resources to tag. For example, <code>ami-1a2b3c4d</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Tags</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#Tag">Tag</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>List of one or more tags. The value parameter is required, but if you donât want the tag to have a value, specify the parameter with no value, and Mule sets the value to an empty string.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

See [Exception Throw List - 1](#exceptionthrows1)

### Create Volume

`<ec2:create-volume>`

Creates an EBS volume to attach to an instance that is in the same Availability Zone. The volume is
created in the regional endpoint to which you send the HTTP request. You can create a new empty
volume or restore a volume from an EBS snapshot. Any AWS Marketplace product codes from the snapshot
are propagated to the volume.

To create encrypted volumes, use the `Encrypted` parameter. Encrypted volumes can be attached only
to instances that support Amazon EBS encryption. Volumes that are created from encrypted snapshots
are automatically encrypted.

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Availability Zone</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The Amazon availability zone in which to create the volume.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Encrypted</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies whether to encrypt the volume.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>false</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Iops</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Only valid for provisioned IOPS SSD volumes. The number of I/O operations per second (IOPS) to provision for the volume, with a maximum ratio of 50 IOPS/GiB.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Kms Key Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Full ARN of the AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) customer key (CMK) to use when creating the encrypted volume.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The size of the volume, in GiBs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Snapshot Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The snapshot from which to create the volume.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Volume Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>Standard</p></li><li><p>Io1</p></li><li><p>Gp2</p></li><li><p>Sc1</p></li><li><p>St1</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The volume type.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Standard</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#Volume">Volume</a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RequestIDAttribute">Request ID Attribute</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

See [Exception Throw List - 1](#exceptionthrows1)

### Delete Key Pair

`<ec2:delete-key-pair>`

Removes the public key from Amazon EC2 and deletes the specified key pair.

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the key pair to delete.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. ### Delete Network Interface

`<ec2:delete-network-interface>`

Deletes the specified network interface. You must detach the network interface before you can delete
it.

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Network Interface Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID of the network interface.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. ### Delete Security Group

`<ec2:delete-security-group>`

Deletes a security group.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Group Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The security group ID is required for nondefault VPC.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Group Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>For EC2-Classic and default VPC, name of the security group.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output.
