#### Throws

See [Exception Throw List - 1](#exceptionthrows1)

### Delete Snapshot

`<ec2:delete-snapshot>`

Deletes the specified snapshot. When you make periodic snapshots of a volume, the snapshots are
incremental, which means that only the blocks that changes on the device since your last snapshot
are saved in the new snapshot. When you delete a snapshot, only the data that is not needed for any
other snapshot is removed. This ensures that, no matter which prior snapshots were deleted, all
active snapshots have access to all the information needed to restore the volume.

You cannot delete a snapshot of the root device of an EBS volume used by a registered AMI. You must
de-register the AMI before you can delete the snapshot.

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Snapshot Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID of the EBS snapshot.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

See [Exception Throw List - 1](#exceptionthrows1)

### Delete Tags

`<ec2:delete-tags>`

Deletes the specified set of tags from the specified set of resources. This call is designed to
follow a `DescribeTags` call so you can first determine what tags a resource has. You can then call
`DeleteTags` with the resource ID and the specific tags you want to delete.

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Resources</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>List of resource IDs, for example: <code>ami-1a2b3c4d</code>. You can specify more than one resource ID.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Tags</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#Tag">Tag</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>List of one or more tags to delete. If you omit the value parameter, Mule deletes the tag regardless of its value. If you specify this parameter with an empty string as the value, Mule deletes the key only if its value is an empty string.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

See [Exception Throw List - 1](#exceptionthrows1)

### Delete Volume

`<ec2:delete-volume>`

Deletes the specified EBS volume. The volume must be in the available state (not attached to an
instance). The volume might remain in the deleting state for several minutes.

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Volume Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID of the volume.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

See [Exception Throw List - 1](#exceptionthrows1)

### Deregister Image

`<ec2:deregister-image>`

Deregisters the specified AMI. Once deregistered, the AMI cannot be used to launch new instances.

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Image Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>AMI ID to deregister.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

See [Exception Throw List - 1](#exceptionthrows1)

### Describe Addresses

`<ec2:describe-addresses>`

Describes one or more of your Elastic IP addresses.

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Allocation Ids</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>For EC2-VPC, one or more allocation IDs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Filters</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#Filter">Filter</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>One or more filters. Filter names and values are case-sensitive.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Public Ips</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>For EC2-Classic, one or more Elastic IP addresses.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Describe Availability Zones

`<ec2:describe-availability-zones>`

Describes one or more of the Availability Zones that are currently available to the account. The
results include only the zones for the region youâre currently using.

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

### Describe Images

`<ec2:describe-images>`

Describes the images in AWS (AMIs, AKIs, and ARIs). Images available to you include public images,
private images that you own, and private images owned by other AWS accounts for which you have
explicit launch permissions.

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Describe Instance Attribute

`<ec2:describe-instance-attribute>`

Describes the specified attribute of the specified instance. You can specify only one attribute at a
time. Valid attribute values are:

- `blockDeviceMapping`
- `disableApiTermination`
- `ebsOptimized`, `groupSet`
- `instanceInitiatedShutdownBehavior`
- `instanceType`
- `kernel`
- `productCodes`
- `ramdisk`
- `rootDeviceName`
- `sourceDestCheck`
- `sriovNetSupport`
- `userData`

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attribute</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Instance attribute.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Instance Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID of the instance.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#InstanceAttribute">Instance Attribute</a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RequestIDAttribute">Request ID Attribute</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

#### Throws

### Describe Instance Status

`<ec2:describe-instance-status>`

Describes the status of one or more instances. By default, only running instances are described,
unless otherwise specified.

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Instance Ids</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>One or more instance IDs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Filters</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#Filter">Filter</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>One or more filters.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Describe Instances

`<ec2:describe-instances>`

Describes one or more of your instances. If you specify one or more instance IDs, Amazon EC2 returns
information for those instances. If you donât specify instance IDs or filters, Amazon EC2 returns
information for all instances, which can have a performance impact. If you specify an invalid
instance ID, an error is returned.

If you specify an instance that you donât own, that instance is not included in the returned
results.

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Instance Ids</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>One or more instance IDs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Filters</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#Filter">Filter</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>One or more filters.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of <a href="#Reservation">Reservation</a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RequestIDAttribute">Request ID Attribute</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

See [Exception Throw List - 1](#exceptionthrows1)

### Describe Key Pairs

`<ec2:describe-key-pairs>`

Describes one or more of your key pairs.

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key Names</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>One or more key pair names for which the describeKeyPairs service needs to return information. When the list is null or empty, it returns all the available key pairs' information.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Filters</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#Filter">Filter</a></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of <a href="#KeyPairInfo">Key Pair Info</a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RequestIDAttribute">Request ID Attribute</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

See [Exception Throw List - 1](#exceptionthrows1)

### Describe Moving Addresses

`<ec2:describe-moving-addresses>`

Describes the Elastic IP addresses to move to the EC2-VPC platform, or to restore to the EC2-Classic
platform. This request does not return information about any other Elastic IP addresses in your
account.

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Filters</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#Filter">Filter</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>One or more filters.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Public Ips</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>One or more Elastic IP addresses.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#repeatable-in-memory-iterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#repeatable-file-store-iterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configure how Mule processes streams with streaming strategies. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of Message of <a href="#MovingAddressStatus">Move Address Status</a> payload and <a href="#RequestIDAttribute">Request ID Attribute</a> attributes</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

See [Exception Throw List - 2](#exceptionthrows2)

### Describe Network Interface Attribute

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attribute</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The attribute of the network interface. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Describe Snapshot Attribute

`<ec2:describe-snapshot-attribute>`

Describes the specified attribute of the specified snapshot. You can specify only one attribute at a
time.

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Snapshot Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID of the EBS snapshot.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attribute</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ProductCodes</p></li><li><p>CreateVolumePermission</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The snapshot attribute to view.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>CreateVolumePermission</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#DescribeSnapshotAttributeResult">Describe Snapshot Attribute Result</a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RequestIDAttribute">Request ID Attribute</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

See [Exception Throw List - 1](#exceptionthrows1)

### Describe Snapshots

`<ec2:describe-snapshots>`

Describes one or more of the EBS snapshots available to you. Available snapshots include:

- Public snapshots available for any AWS account to launch
- Private snapshots that you own
- Private snapshots owned by another AWS account but for which you have explicit create volume
  permissions

The list of snapshots returned can be modified by specifying snapshot IDs, snapshot owners, or AWS
accounts with create volume permissions. If no options are specified, Amazon EC2 returns all
snapshots for which you have create volume permissions.

If you specify:

- One or more snapshot IDs, only snapshots that have the specified IDs are returned.
- An invalid snapshot ID, an error is returned.
- A snapshot ID for which you do not have access, it is not included in the returned results.
- One or more snapshot owners using the OwnerIds option, only snapshots from the specified owners
  and for which you have access are returned.  
  The results can include the AWS account IDs of the specified owners: `amazon` for snapshots owned
  by Amazon, or `self` for snapshots that you own.
- A list of restorable users, only snapshots with create snapshot permissions for those users are
  returned.

You can specify AWS account IDs (if you own the snapshots): `self` for snapshots you own or to which
you have explicit permissions, or `all` for public snapshots.

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Snapshot Ids</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>One or more snapshot IDs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Owner Ids</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Returns the snapshots owned by the specified owner.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Restorable By User Ids</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>One or more AWS accounts IDs that can create volumes from the snapshot.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Filters</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#Filter">Filter</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>One or more filters.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#repeatable-in-memory-iterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#repeatable-file-store-iterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configure how Mule processes streams with streaming strategies. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of Message of <a href="#Snapshot">Snapshot</a> payload and <a href="#RequestIDAttribute">Request ID Attribute</a> attributes</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

See [Exception Throw List - 2](#exceptionthrows2)

### Describe Stale Security Groups

`<ec2:describe-stale-security-groups>`

(EC2-VPC only) Describes the stale security group rules for security groups in a specified VPC.

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Vpc Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The ID of the VPC.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#repeatable-in-memory-iterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#repeatable-file-store-iterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configure how Mule processes streams with streaming strategies. ### Describe Volume Attribute

`<ec2:describe-volume-attribute>`

Describes the specified attribute of the specified volume. You can specify only one attribute at a
time.

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Volume Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The ID of the volume.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attribute Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AutoEnableIO</p></li><li><p>ProductCodes</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The attribute of the volume.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>AutoEnableIO</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. ### Describe Volume Status

`<ec2:describe-volume-status>`

Describes the status of the specified volumes and provides the result of the checks performed on
your volumes to determine events that can impair the performance of your volumes.

If an issue occurs on the volumeâs underlying host, volume performance can be affected. If the
volumeâs underlying host experiences a power outage or system issue, after the system is restored,
data inconsistencies on the volume are possible. Volume events notify you if this occurs. Volume
actions notify you if any action must be taken in response to the event.

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Volume Ids</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>One or more volume IDs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Filters</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#Filter">Filter</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>One or more filters.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#repeatable-in-memory-iterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#repeatable-file-store-iterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configure how Mule processes streams with streaming strategies. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of Message of <a href="#VolumeStatusItem">Volume Status Item</a> payload and <a href="#RequestIDAttribute">Request ID Attribute</a> attributes</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

See [Exception Throw List - 2](#exceptionthrows2)

### Describe Volumes

`<ec2:describe-volumes>`

Describes the specified EBS volumes. If you are describing a long list of volumes, you can paginate
the output to make the list more manageable. The `MaxResults` parameter sets the maximum number of
results returned in a single page. If the list of results exceeds your `MaxResults` value, then that
number of results is returned along with a `NextToken` value that can be passed to a subsequent
`DescribeVolumes` request to retrieve the remaining results.

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Volume Ids</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>One or more volume IDs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Filters</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#Filter">Filter</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>One or more filters.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#repeatable-in-memory-iterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#repeatable-file-store-iterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configure how Mule processes streams with streaming strategies. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of Message of <a href="#Volume">Volume</a> payload and <a href="#RequestIDAttribute">Request ID Attribute</a> attributes</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

See [Exception Throw List - 2](#exceptionthrows2)

### Describe Volumes Modifications

`<ec2:describe-volumes-modifications>`

Reports the current modification status of EBS volumes. Current-generation EBS volumes support
modification of attributes including type, size, and (for io1 volumes) IOPS provisioning while
either attached to or detached from an instance. Following an action from the API or the console to
modify a volume, the status of the modification can be modifying, optimizing, completed, or failed.
If a volume has never been modified, then certain elements of the returned VolumeModification
objects are null.

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Volume Ids</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>One or more volume IDs for which in-progress modifications will be described.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Filters</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#Filter">Filter</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>One or more filters.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#repeatable-in-memory-iterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#repeatable-file-store-iterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configure how Mule processes streams with streaming strategies. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of Message of <a href="#VolumeModification">Volume Modification</a> payload and <a href="#RequestIDAttribute">Request ID Attribute</a> attributes</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

See [Exception Throw List - 2](#exceptionthrows2)

### Detach Network Interface

`<ec2:detach-network-interface>`

Detaches a network interface from an instance.

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attachment Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The ID of the network interface.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Force</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies whether to force a detachment.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>false</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

See [Exception Throw List - 1](#exceptionthrows1)

### Detach Volume

`<ec2:detach-volume>`

Detaches an EBS volume from an instance. You must unmount any file systems on the device within your
operating system before detaching the volume. Failure to do so can result in the volume becoming
stuck in the busy state while detaching. If this happens, detachment can be delayed indefinitely
until you unmount the volume, force detachment, reboot the instance, or all three.

If an EBS volume is the root device of an instance, it canât be detached while the instance is
running. To detach the root volume, stop the instance first. When a volume with an AWS Marketplace
product code is detached from an instance, the product code is no longer associated with the
instance.

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Volume Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The ID of the volume.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Device</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The device name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Force</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Forces detachment if the previous detachment attempt did not occur cleanly (for example, logging into an instance, unmounting the volume, and detaching normally).</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>false</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Instance Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The ID of the instance.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#VolumeAttachment">Volume Attachment</a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RequestIDAttribute">Request ID Attribute</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

See [Exception Throw List - 1](#exceptionthrows1)

### Disassociate Address

`<ec2:disassociate-address>`

Disassociates an Elastic IP address from the instance or network interface itâs associated with.

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Association Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Association ID. Required for EC2-Classic.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Enable Volume I/O

`<ec2:enable-volume-io>`

Enables input and output operations for a volume that had I/O operations disabled because the data
on the volume was potentially inconsistent.

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Volume Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID of the volume.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. ### Get Console Screenshot

`<ec2:get-console-screenshot>`

Retrieve a JPG-format screenshot of a running instance to help with troubleshooting. The returned
content is Base64-encoded.

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Instance Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID of the instance.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. ### Get Password Data

`<ec2:get-password-data>`

Retrieves the encrypted administrator password for an instance running Windows.

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Instance Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The ID of the Windows instance.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Import Key Pair

`<ec2:import-key-pair>` Imports the public key from an RSA key pair that you created with a
third-party tool. Compare this with CreateKeyPair, in which AWS creates the key pair and gives the
keys to you (AWS keeps a copy of the public key). With ImportKeyPair, you create the key pair and
give AWS just the public key. The private key is never transferred between you and AWS.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A unique name for the key pair.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Public Key Material</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The public key which must be base64-encoded.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#ImportKeyPairResult">Import Key Pair Result</a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RequestIDAttribute">Request ID Attribute</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

See [Exception Throw List - 1](#exceptionthrows1)

### Modify Image Attribute

`<ec2:modify-image-attribute>`

Modifies the specified attribute of the specified AMI. You can specify only one attribute at a time.

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Image Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID of the AMI.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Description</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Description of the AMI.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attribute</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the attribute to modify.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Operation Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Type of operation.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Launch Permissions</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#LaunchPermission">Launch Permission</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Launch permissions for the AMI.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Product Codes</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The DevPay product codes. After you add a product code to an AMI, it canât be removed.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Value of the attribute to modify.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

See [Exception Throw List - 1](#exceptionthrows1)

### Modify Instance Attribute

`<ec2:modify-instance-attribute>`

Modifies the specified attribute of the specified instance. You can specify only one attribute at a
time. To modify some attributes, the instance must be stopped.

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Instance Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID of the instance.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attribute</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the attribute.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>New value for the attribute.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

See [Exception Throw List - 1](#exceptionthrows1)

### Modify Network Interface Attribute

`<ec2:modify-network-interface-attribute>`

Modifies the specified network interface attribute. You can specify only one attribute at a time. Be
careful while using this operation.

- To modify only the **Description** attribute, pass only the description along with **Network
  Interface Id**.
- To modify **Attachment Id**, only send **Attachment Id** and **Delete On Termination** set to
  `false`.
- To modify **Groups**, send `groupSet` along with **Network Interface Id**.

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Network Interface Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID of the network interface.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attachment Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID of the network interface attachment.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Description</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Description for the network interface.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Groups</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Changes the security groups for the network interface. The new set of groups you specify replaces the current set. You must specify at least one group, even if itâs just the default security group in the VPC. You must specify the ID of the security group, not the name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Delete On Termination</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates whether the network interface is deleted when the instance is terminated.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>false</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Source Dest Check</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates whether source or destination checking is enabled. A value of <code>true</code> means checking is enabled, and <code>false</code> means checking is disabled. This value must be <code>false</code> for a NAT instance to perform NAT.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>false</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

See [Exception Throw List - 1](#exceptionthrows1)

### Modify Snapshot Attribute

`<ec2:modify-snapshot-attribute>`

Adds or removes permission settings for the specified snapshot. You can add or remove specified AWS
account IDs from a snapshotâs list of create volume permissions, but you cannot do both in a single
API call. If you need to both add and remove account IDs for a snapshot, you must use multiple API
calls.

You canât make encrypted snapshots and snapshots with AWS Marketplace product codes public.
Snapshots encrypted with your default CMK cannot be shared with other accounts.

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Snapshot Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID of the snapshot.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attribute</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ProductCodes</p></li><li><p>CreateVolumePermission</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The snapshot attribute to modify.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>CreateVolumePermission</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Operation Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>Add</p></li><li><p>Remove</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Type of operation to perform to the attribute.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Add</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Group Names</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Group to modify for the snapshot.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>User Ids</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Account ID to modify for the snapshot.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>
