### Modify Volume

`<ec2:modify-volume>`

You can modify several parameters of an existing EBS volume, including volume size, volume type, and
IOPS capacity. If your EBS volume is attached to a current-generation EC2 instance type, it might be
possible to apply these changes without stopping the instance or detaching the volume from it. When
you complete a resize operation on your volume, you need to extend the volumeâs file-system size to
take advantage of the new storage capacity.

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Volume Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID of the volume to modify.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Iops</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Target IOPS rate of the volume to modify.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Target size in GiB of the volume to modify.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Volume Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>Standard</p></li><li><p>Io1</p></li><li><p>Gp2</p></li><li><p>Sc1</p></li><li><p>St1</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Target EBS volume type of the volume to be modified</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#VolumeModification">Volume Modification</a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RequestIDAttribute">Request ID Attribute</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

See [Exception Throw List - 1](#exceptionthrows1)

### Modify Volume Attribute

`<ec2:modify-volume-attribute>`

Modifies a volume attribute. By default, all I/O operations for the volume are suspended when the
data on the volume is determined to be potentially inconsistent. This behavior prevents undetectable
latent data corruption. The I/O access to the volume can be resumed by first enabling I/O access and
then checking the data consistency on your volume.

You can change the default behavior to resume I/O operations. It is best to change the default
behavior only for boot volumes or for volumes that are stateless or disposable.

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Volume Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID of the volume.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Auto Enable IO</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates whether the volume should be auto-enabled for I/O operations.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>true</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

See [Exception Throw List - 1](#exceptionthrows1)

### Monitor Instances

`<ec2:monitor-instances>`

Enables detailed monitoring for a running instance. Otherwise, basic monitoring is enabled.

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Instance Ids</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>One or more instance IDs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

### Move Address To VPC

`<ec2:move-address-to-vpc>`

Moves an Elastic IP address from the EC2-Classic platform to the EC2-VPC platform. The Elastic IP
address must be allocated to your account for more than 24 hours, and it must not be associated with
an instance.

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Public Ip</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The Elastic IP address.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#MoveAddressToVpcResult">Move Address To VPC Result</a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RequestIDAttribute">Request ID Attribute</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

See [Exception Throw List - 1](#exceptionthrows1)

### Reboot Instances

`<ec2:reboot-instances>`

Requests a reboot of one or more instances. This operation is asynchronous, meaning that it only
queues a request to reboot the specified instances. The operation succeeds if the instances are
valid and belong to you. Requests to reboot terminated instances are ignored. If an instance does
not cleanly shut down within four minutes, Amazon EC2 performs a hard reboot.

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Instance Ids</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>One or more instance IDs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

See [Exception Throw List - 1](#exceptionthrows1)

### Release Address

`<ec2:release-address>`

Releases the specified Elastic IP address. After an Elastic IP address is released, it is sent to
the IP address pool and might be unavailable to you.

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Allocation Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Required allocation ID for EC2-VPC.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Public Ip</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Required Elastic IP address for EC2-Classic.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

See [Exception Throw List - 1](#exceptionthrows1)

### Replace IAM Instance Profile Association

`<ec2:replace-iam-instance-profile-association>`

Replaces an IAM instance profile for the specified running instance. You can use this action to
change the IAM instance profile thatâs associated with an instance without having to disassociate
the existing IAM instance profile first.

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Association Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID of the existing IAM instance profile association</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Profile Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the instance profile to replace.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#IamInstanceProfileAssociation">IAM Instance Profile Association</a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RequestIDAttribute">Request ID Attribute</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

### Report Instance Status

`<ec2:report-instance-status>`

Submits feedback about the status of an instance. The instance must be in the running state.

If your experience with the instance differs from the instance status returned by
`DescribeInstanceStatus`, use `ReportInstanceStatus` to report your experience with the instance.
Amazon EC2 collects this information to improve the accuracy of status checks. Use of this action
does not change the value returned by DescribeInstanceStatus.

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Instance Ids</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>One or more instance IDs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reason Codes</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>One or more reason codes that describes the health state of your instance.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Status</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The status of all instances listed.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Description</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Descriptive text about the health state of your instance.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

See [Exception Throw List - 1](#exceptionthrows1)

### Reset Image Attribute

`<ec2:reset-image-attribute>`

Resets an attribute of an AMI to its default value.

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Image Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID of the AMI.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attribute</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Attribute to reset. Currently, you can only reset the launch permission attribute).</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. ### Reset Instance Attribute

`<ec2:reset-instance-attribute>`

Resets an attribute of an instance to its default value. To reset the kernel or RAM disk, the
instance must be in a stopped state. To reset the `sourceDestCheck`, the instance can be either
running or stopped. The `sourceDestCheck` attribute controls whether source or destination checking
is enabled. The default value is `true`, which means checking is enabled. This value must be `false`
for a NAT instance to perform NAT.

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Instance Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID of the instance.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attribute</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Attribute to reset. You can reset only the following attributes:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>kernel</code></p></li><li><p><code>ramdisk</code></p></li><li><p><code>sourceDestCheck</code></p></li></ul></div><div><p>To change an instance attribute, use <code>ModifyInstanceAttribute</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

See [Exception Throw List - 1](#exceptionthrows1)

### Reset Network Interface Attribute

`<ec2:reset-network-interface-attribute>`

Resets a network interface attribute. You can specify only one attribute at a time.

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Network Interface Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID of the network interface.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Source Dest Check</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Source or destination checking attribute. Resets the value to <code>true</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reset Snapshot Attribute

`<ec2:reset-snapshot-attribute>`

Resets permission settings for the specified snapshot.

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Snapshot Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID of the snapshot.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attribute</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ProductCodes</p></li><li><p>CreateVolumePermission</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The attribute to reset.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>CreateVolumePermission</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. ### Restore Address To Classic

`<ec2:restore-address-to-classic>`

Restores an elastic IP address that was previously moved to the EC2-VPC platform back to the
EC2-Classic platform.

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Public Ip</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Elastic IP address.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. ### Revoke Security Group Egress

`<ec2:revoke-security-group-egress>`

(EC2-VPC only) Removes one or more egress rules from a security group for EC2-VPC.

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Group Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID of the security group.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Cidr Ip</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>CIDR IP address range.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Ip Protocol</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>IP protocol name or number.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>From Port</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Start of port range for the TCP and UDP protocols, or an ICMP type number.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>To Port</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>End of port range for the TCP and UDP protocols, or an ICMP type number.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Ip Permissions</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#IpPermission">IP Permission</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A set of IP permissions.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Source Security Group Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of a destination security group.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Source Security Group Owner Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>AWS account number for a destination security group.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

### Revoke Security Group Ingress

`<ec2:revoke-security-group-ingress>`

Removes one or more ingress rules from a security group.

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Group Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID of the security group.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Group Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>EC2-Classic, default VPC - The name of the security group.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Cidr Ip</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>CIDR IP address range.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Ip Protocol</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>IP protocol name (<code>tcp</code>, <code>udp</code>, <code>icmp</code>) or number.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>From Port</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Start of port range for the TCP and UDP protocols, or an ICMP type number.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>To Port</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The end of port range for the TCP and UDP protocols, or an ICMP code number.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Ip Permissions</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#IpPermission">IP Permission</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A set of IP permissions.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Source Security Group Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>For EC2-Classic, default VPC, this is the name of the source security group.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Source Security Group Owner Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>For EC2-Classic, this is the AWS account ID of the source security group, if the source security group is in a different account.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

See [Exception Throw List - 1](#exceptionthrows1)

### Run Instances

`<ec2:run-instances>`

Launches the specified number of instances of an AMI for which you have permissions.

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Image Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID of the AMI, which you can get by calling describe-images.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Min Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Minimum number of instances to launch.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>1</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum number of instances to launch.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>1</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Security Groups</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>One or more security group names.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Security Group Ids</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>One or more security group IDs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Keyname</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the key pair.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Subnet Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID of the subnet to launch the instance into.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Instance Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#Reservation">Reservation</a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RequestIDAttribute">Request ID Attribute</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

See [Exception Throw List - 1](#exceptionthrows1)

### Start Instances

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

### Terminate Instances

`<ec2:terminate-instances>`

Shuts down one or more instances. This operation is idempotent; if you terminate an instance more
than once, each call succeeds. Terminated instances remain visible after termination (approximately
one hour).

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Instance Ids</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>One or more instance IDs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of <a href="#InstanceStateChange">Instance State Change</a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RequestIDAttribute">Request ID Attribute</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

See [Exception Throw List - 1](#exceptionthrows1)

### Unassign IPv6 Addresses

`<ec2:unassign-ipv6-addresses>`

Unassigns one or more IPv6 addresses from a network interface.

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Ipv6 Addresses</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>IPv6 addresses to unassign from the network interface.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Network Interface Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID of the network interface.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Unassign Private IP Addresses

`<ec2:unassign-private-ip-addresses>`

Unassigns one or more secondary private IP addresses from a network interface.

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Network Interface Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID of the network interface.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Private Ip Addresses</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Secondary private IP addresses to unassign from the network interface. You can specify this option multiple times to unassign more than one IP address.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Unmonitor Instances

`<ec2:unmonitor-instances>`

Disables detailed monitoring for a running instance.

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the following parameters for this operation:

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Instance Ids</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>One or more instance IDs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of <a href="#InstanceMonitoring">Instance Monitoring</a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RequestIDAttribute">Request ID Attribute</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

See [Exception Throw List - 1](#exceptionthrows1)

## Types

Amazon EC2 Connector supports only the types that are documented.

### TLS

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Enabled Protocols</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A comma-separated list of protocols enabled for this context.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Enabled Cipher Suites</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A comma-separated list of cipher suites enabled for this context.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Trust Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#TrustStore">Trust Store</a></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#KeyStore">Key Store</a></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Revocation Check</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#standard-revocation-check">Standard Revocation Check</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#custom-ocsp-responder">Custom OCSP Responder</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#crl-file">CRL File</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Trust Store

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The location of the truststore, which is resolved relative to the current classpath and file system, if possible.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The password used to protect the truststore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The type of truststore used.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The algorithm used by the truststore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Insecure</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If <code>true</code>, no certificate validations are performed, which makes connections vulnerable to attacks. Use at your own risk.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Key Store

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The location of the keystore, which is resolved relative to the current classpath and file system, if possible.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The type of keystore used.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Alias</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>When the keystore contains many private keys, this attribute indicates the alias of the key that should be used. If not defined, the first key in the file will be used by default.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The password used to protect the private key.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The password used to protect the keystore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The algorithm used by the keystore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Standard Revocation Check

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Only End Entities</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Only verify the last element of the certificate chain.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Prefer Crls</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Try CRL instead of OCSP first.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>No Fallback</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Do not use the secondary checking method (the one not selected before).</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Soft Fail</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Avoid verification failure when the revocation server can not be reached or is busy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Custom OCSP Responder

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Url</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The URL of the OCSP responder.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Cert Alias</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Alias of the signing certificate for the OCSP response (must be in the trust store), if present.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### CRL File

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The path to the CRL file.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnection

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Fails Deployment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The reconnection strategy to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Frequency</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How often to reconnect (in milliseconds).</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The number of reconnection attempts to make.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect Forever

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Frequency</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How often in milliseconds to reconnect.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Expiration Policy

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Max Idle Time</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A scalar time value for the maximum amount of time a dynamic configuration instance can be idle before itâs eligible for expiration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A time unit that qualifies the maxIdleTime attribute.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Allocate Address Result

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Allocation Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Assigned allocation ID that represents the allocation of the Elastic IP address used with instances in a VPC.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Domain</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies whether to use the Elastic IP address with instances in a VPC (<code>vpc</code>) or in EC2-Class (<code>standard</code>).</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Public Ip</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Elastic IP address.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>
