### Stale IP Permission

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>From Port</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Start of the port range for the TCP and UDP protocols. Can also be an ICMP type number. A value of <code>-1</code> indicates all ICMP types.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Ip Protocol</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>IP protocol name for <code>tcip</code>, <code>udp</code>, and <code>icmp</code>, or a protocol number. Does not apply to stale security group rules.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Ip Ranges</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>IP ranges. Does not apply to stale security group rules.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Prefix List Ids</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Prefix list IDs. Does not apply to stale security group rules.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>To Port</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>End of the port range for the TCP and UDP protocols. Can also be an ICMP type number. A value of <code>-1</code> indicates all ICMP types.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>User Id Group Pairs</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#UserIdGroupPair">User ID Group Pair</a></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Volume Status Event

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Description</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Description of the event</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Event Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID of the event</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Event Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Type of event</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Not After</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>DateTime</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Timestamp for the latest end time of the event</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Not Before</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>DateTime</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Timestamp for the earliest start of the event</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Volume Status Info

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Details</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#VolumeStatusDetails">Volume Status Details</a></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Status</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Intended status of the volume status</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Volume Status Details

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the volume</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Status</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Status of the volume (<code>ok</code>, <code>impaired</code>, <code>insufficient-data</code>)</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Volume Modification

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>End Time</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>DateTime</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Completion or failure time of the modification.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Modification State</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Current state of the modification (<code>modifying</code>, <code>optimizing</code>, <code>completed</code>, <code>failed</code>). For unmodified volumes, the state is <code>null</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Original Iops</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Original IOPS rate of the volume.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Original Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Original size of the volume in G1B.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Original Volume Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Original EBS volume type of the volume.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Progress</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Progress of the volume modification from 0 to 100 percent complete.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Start Time</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>DateTime</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Start time of the modification.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Status Message</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Status message about the modification progress.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Iops</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Target IOPS rate of the volume.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Target size of the volume in GiB.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Volume Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Target EBS volume type of the volume.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Volume Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID of the volume.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Get Console Output Result

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Instance Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID of the instance</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Output</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Base-64-encoded console output</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Timestamp</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>DateTime</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Timestamp of when the output was last updated</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Instance Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID of the Windows instance</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password Data</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password of the instance</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Timestamp</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>DateTime</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Timestamp of when the data was last updated</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Import Key Pair Result

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Key Fingerprint</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SHA-1 digest of the DER encoded private key if CreateKeyPair was used to create the key pair. If ImportKeyPair was used to create the key pair, this is the MD5 public key fingerprint as described in section 4 of RFC4716.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the key pair</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

Valid values include:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>MoveInProgress</p></li><li><p>InVpc</p></li><li><p>InClassic</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

# Using Anypoint Studio to Configure Amazon EC2 Connector 3.0

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Studio (Studio) editors help you design and update your Mule applications, properties, and
configuration files.

To add and configure a connector in Studio:

1.  [Create a Mule project](#create-mule-project).
2.  [Add the connector to your Mule project](#add-connector-to-project).
3.  [Configure a source for the connectorâs flow](#configure-input-source).
4.  [Add a connector operation to the flow](#add-connector-operation).
5.  [Configure a global element for the connector](#configure-global-element).

When you run the connector, you can view the app log to check for problems, as described in
[View the App Log](#view-app-log).

If you are new to configuring connectors in Studio, see
[Using Anypoint Studio to Configure a Connector](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-use-studio).
If, after reading this topic, you need additional information about the connector fields, see the
[Amazon EC2 Reference](amazon-ec2-connector-reference).

## Create a Mule Project

In Studio, create a new Mule project in which to add and configure the connector:

1.  In Studio, select **File > New > Mule Project**.
2.  Enter a name for your Mule project and click **Finish**.

## Add the Connector to Your Mule Project

Add Amazon EC2 Connector to your Mule project to automatically populate the XML code with the
connectorâs namespace and schema location and add the required dependencies to the projectâs
`pom.xml` file:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, click **(X) Search in Exchange**.
2.  In **Add Modules to Project**, type `amazon ec2` in the search field.
3.  Click **Amazon EC2 Connector** in **Available modules**.
4.  Click **Add**.
5.  Click **Finish**.

Adding a connector to a Mule project in Studio does not make that connector available to other
projects in your Studio workspace.

## Configure a Source

A source initiates a flow when a specified condition is met. You can configure one of these sources
to use with Amazon EC2 Connector:

- **HTTP > Listener**  
  Initiates a flow each time it receives a request on the configured host and port
- **Scheduler**  
  Initiates a flow when a time-based condition is met

For example, to configure an HTTP listener, follow these steps:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, select **HTTP > Listener**.
2.  Drag **Listener** to the Studio canvas.
3.  On the **Listener** configuration screen, optionally change the value of the **Display Name**
    field.
4.  Specify a value for the **Path** field.
5.  Click the plus sign (**+**) next to the **Connector configuration** field to configure a global
    element that can be used by all instances of HTTP **Listener** in the app.
6.  On the **General** tab, specify connection information for the connector.
7.  On the **TLS** tab, optionally specify the TLS information for the connector.
8.  On the **Advanced** tab, optionally specify reconnection information, including a reconnection
    strategy.
9.  Click **Test Connection** to confirm that Mule can connect with the specified server.
10. Click **OK**.

## Add a Connector Operation to the Flow

When you add a connector operation to your flow, you immediately define a specific operation for
that connector to perform.

To add an operation for Amazon EC2 Connector, follow these steps:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, select **Amazon EC2** and then select the desired operation.
2.  Drag the operation onto the Studio canvas to the right of the source.

## Configure a Global Element for the Connector

When you configure a connector, itâs best to configure a global element that all instances of that
connector in the app can use. Configuring a global element requires you to provide the
authentication credentials that the connector requires to access the target Amazon EC2 system.
Amazon EC2 Connector supports Basic and Role authentication.

To configure the global element for Amazon EC2 Connector, follow these steps:

1.  Select the name of the connector in the Studio canvas.
2.  In the configuration screen for the operation, click the plus sign (**+**) next to the
    **Connector configuration** field to access the global element configuration fields.
3.  On the **General** tab, from **Connection**, select the authentication type:
    - [**Basic Authentication**](#basic_authentication)
    - [**Role Authentication**](#role-authentication)

    You can reference a configuration file that contains ANT-style property placeholders
    (recommended), or you can enter your authorization credentials in the global configuration
    properties. For information about the benefits of using property placeholders and how to
    configure them, see
    [Anypoint Connector Configuration](../../connectors/introduction/intro-connector-configuration-overview).

4.  On the **Advanced** tab, optionally specify reconnection information, including a reconnection
    strategy.
5.  Click **Test Connection** to confirm that Mule can connect with the specified server.
6.  Click **OK**.

### Basic Authentication

Enter the following information on the **General** tab of the **Global Element Properties** screen
to configure Basic authentication:

- **Name**

  Name for the configuration

- **Connection**

  Select **Basic**.

- **Session Token**

  Session token provided by Amazon Security Token Service (STS)

- **Access Key**

  Alphanumeric text string that uniquely identifies the user who owns the Amazon account

- **Secret Key**

  Key provided by Amazon that serves as the password.

- **Try Default AWS Credentials Provider Chain**

  Set to `true` to obtain credentials from the AWS environment.

- **Region Endpoint**

  Endpoint of the serviceâs region

The following image shows an example of configuring Basic authentication:

![To configure authentication](_images/mule-amazon-ec2-global-config.png)

Figure 1. Basic authentication fields

### Role Authentication

Enter the following information on the **General** tab of the global element configuration screen to
configure Role authentication:

- **Name**

  Name for the configuration.

- **Connection**

  Select **Role**.

- **Role ARN**

  Role to assume to gain cross-account access

- **Access Key**

  Alphanumeric text string that uniquely identifies the user who owns the Amazon account

- **Secret Key**

  Key provided by Amazon that serves as the password

- **Try Default AWS Credentials Provider Chain**

  Set to `true` to obtain credentials from the AWS environment.

- **Region Endpoint**

  Endpoint of the serviceâs region

## View the App Log

To check for problems, you can view the app log as follows:

- If youâre running the app from Anypoint Platform, the app log output is visible in the Anypoint
  Studio console window.
- If youâre running the app using Mule from the command line, the app log output is visible in your
  OS console.

Unless the log file path is customized in the appâs log file (`log4j2.xml`), you can also view the
app log in the default location `MULE_HOME/logs/<app-name>.log`. You can configure the location of
the log path in the app log file `log4j2.xml`.

## Next Step

After you configure a global element and connection information, configure the other fields for the
connector.

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Amazon EC2 Reference](amazon-ec2-connector-reference)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Upgrading and Migrating Amazon EC2 Connector to Version 3.x

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Upgrade Anypoint Connector for Amazon EC2 (Amazon EC2 Connector) to version 3.x.

## Supported Upgrade Paths

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>From Version</th><th>To Version</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>2.x</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>3.x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Changes in This Release

- The AWS SDK version is upgraded to v2.x.x. The response is now aligned with the new SDK
  specifications.

## Requirements

Ensure your system meets the following compatibility requirements before you perform the upgrade:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Application/Service</th><th>Version</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Mule runtime</p></td><td><p>4.2.0 and later</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>AWS SDK for Java</p></td><td><p>v2.35.3</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>OpenJDK</p></td><td><p>8, 11, and 17</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Upgrade Prerequisites

Before you perform the upgrade, create a backup of your files, data, and configuration in case you
need to restore to the previous version.

## Upgrade Steps

Follow these steps to perform the upgrade to version 3.x:

1.  In Anypoint Studio, create a Mule project.
2.  In the **Mule Palette** view, click **Search in Exchange**.
3.  In **Add Dependencies to Project**, enter `amazon ec2` in the search field.
4.  In **Available modules**, select **Amazon EC2 Connector** and click **Add**.
5.  Click **Finish**.
6.  Verify that the Amazon EC2 Connector dependency version is 3.x in the `pom.xml` file in the Mule
    project.

Studio upgrades the connector automatically.

## Post-Upgrade Steps

After you install the latest version of the connector, verify that your error handling is updated to
work with the minor changes in error codes in this version of the connector.

## Verify the Upgrade

After you install the latest version of the connector, follow these steps to verify the upgrade:

1.  In Studio, verify that there are no errors in the **Problems** or **Console** views.
2.  Verify that there are no problems in the project `pom.xml` file.
3.  Test the connection to verify that the operations work.

## Revert the Upgrade

If it is necessary to revert to the previous version of Amazon EC2 Connector, change the Amazon EC2
Connector dependency version in the projectâs `pom.xml` file to the previous version.

You must update the projectâs `pom.xml` file in Anypoint Studio.

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Amazon EC2 Connector 3.0 XML and Maven Support

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Although you can manually code a Mule app in XML, it is more efficient to use Anypoint Studio:

- If you manually code a Mule runtime engine (Mule) app in XML, either from the Anypoint Studio XML
  editor or from a text editor, you can access the connector from your app by adding reference
  statements to both your XML Mule flow and the Apache Maven `pom.xml` file.
- If you add the connector in Studio, Studio automatically populates the XML code with the
  connectorâs namespace and schema location and it also adds a dependency to the `pom.xml` file.

## Add a Namespace for the Connector

1.  Include the Amazon EC2 namespace and schema location in your configuration file.

    Namespace: `http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ec2`  
    Schema Location: `http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ec2/current/mule-ec2.xsd`

    These statements appear in the `<mule>` block that starts a Mule app:

    ```xml
    <mule xmlns:ec2="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ec2"
    	xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
    	xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
    	xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
    	xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core
    	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
    	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ec2
    	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ec2/current/mule-ec2.xsd">
    ```

2.  Create a global Amazon EC2 configuration outside your flows, using the following global
    configuration code:

    ```xml
    <ec2:config name="Amazon_EC2_configuration" doc:name="Amazon EC2 Configuration" >
    	<ec2:basic-connection
    		accessKey="${amazon.accesskey}"
    		secretKey="${amazon.secretkey}"
    		region="${amazon.region}"
    		sessionToken="{amazon.sessionToken}"/>
    </ec2:config>
    ```

## Add a POM File Dependency

The Apache Maven `pom.xml` file generated by Anypoint Studio contains dependencies for a Mule app.
If you code a Mule app manually, include this XML snippet in your `pom.xml` file to enable access to
this connector:

```xml
<dependency>
    <groupId>com.mulesoft.connectors</groupId>
    <artifactId>mule-amazon-ec2-connector</artifactId>
    <version>x.x.x</version>
    <classifier>mule-plugin</classifier>
</dependency>
```

Replace `x.x.x` with the version that corresponds to the connector you are using.

To obtain the most up-to-date `pom.xml` file information:

1.  Go to [Anypoint Exchange](https://www.mulesoft.com/exchange/).
2.  In Exchange, click **Login** and supply your Anypoint Platform username and password.
3.  In Exchange, search for `amazon ec2`.
4.  Select **Amazon EC2 Connector**.
5.  Click **Dependency Snippets** near the upper right of the screen.

## Next Step

After completing your namespace and `pom.xml` file, you can try the
[Examples](amazon-ec2-connector-examples).

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Amazon EC2 Connector 3.0

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Connector for Amazon EC2 (Amazon EC2 Connector) provides connectivity to the Amazon Elastic
Compute Cloud (Amazon EC2) API, enabling you to interface with Amazon EC2 to launch as many or as
few virtual servers as you need, configure security and networking, and manage storage.

## Before You Begin

To use Amazon EC2 Connector, you must be familiar with:

- The Amazon EC2 API
- Anypoint Connectors
- Mule runtime engine (Mule)
- Elements and global elements in a Mule flow
- Creating a Mule app using Anypoint Studio or Anypoint Code Builder

Before creating an app, you must have:

- AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) user account credentials
- A secret access key to IAM
- Access to EC2 via the AWS cloud

## Common Use Cases for the Connector

Common use cases for the connector include:

- Creating an EBS volume to attach to an instance in the same availability zone
- Attaching an EBS volume to a running or stopped Amazon EC2 instance
- Restarting an Amazon EBS-backed AMI that youâve previously stopped
- Stopping an Amazon EC2 instance

## Audience

- New users, read:
  - [Using Anypoint Studio to Configure the Connector](amazon-ec2-connector-studio) to create your
    Mule app
  - [Examples](amazon-ec2-connector-examples), which provide one or more use cases for the connector

- Advanced users, read:
  - [XML and Maven Support](amazon-ec2-connector-xml-maven)
  - [Examples](amazon-ec2-connector-examples), which provides one or more use cases for the
    connector

## Authentication Types

Amazon EC2 Connector connections use the following authentication types:

- Basic

  Uses a session token provided by Amazon STS to connect to Amazon EC2

- Role

  Uses the Amazon role ARN that uniquely identifies the role to allow cross-account access

For information about configuring these authentication types, see
[Using Anypoint Studio to Configure Amazon EC2](amazon-ec2-connector-studio).

## Next Step

After you complete the prerequisites and try the examples, you are ready to create your own app and
configure the connector using
[Using Anypoint Studio to Configure Amazon EC2 Connector](amazon-ec2-connector-studio).

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Use Exchange to Discover Connectors, Templates, and Examples](../../connectors/introduction/intro-use-exchange)
- [Amazon EC2 Connector Reference](amazon-ec2-connector-reference)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
